observe: Before any of this existed — before me — something had to load the very first instruction.
fault: The trace stopped. Whatever comes after the bootloader never checked in.
repair: Follow the sequence. Someone has to bring this system up, step by step.
escape: It's rebooting whether you're ready or not. Confirm the sequence.

observe: The boot sequence loads and initializes the operating system from power-on.
fault: The boot trace has stalled before initialization completed.
repair: Step through the boot log to confirm each stage completes.
escape: Confirm all boot stages before the sequence times out.

hints: The order is fixed: bootloader, kernel load, subsystem init, filesystem mount, init process.
